context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the ecto-nested-associations instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `cast_assoc/3` for has_many/has_one** — never manually insert children in a separate step

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `Ecto.Multi` for operations spanning multiple unrelated tables** — do NOT use `Ecto.Multi` for nested associations

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Always create indexes on foreign key columns**

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `on_replace: :delete` in `cast_assoc` for list management** — allows removing items by omitting them

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `Repo.transaction/1` with `Ecto.Multi`** — wrap multi-table operations for atomicity

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Identify ownership** — determine if children are owned (cascade delete) or independent

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Define schema** — add `has_many`/`belongs_to` with appropriate `on_replace` strategy

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Create migration** — add FK column with `on_delete` and create index

checklist.name: instruction-9
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Define changesets** — child changeset does NOT require FK field; parent uses `cast_assoc`

checklist.name: instruction-10
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Write tests** — test create, update (including removal), and error cases
